body.u: God it seems so long ago when I left school. Actually it was May 26th 1978. The following day I signed on the dole.

I was waiting to join up but of course I couldn’t do that until I was 17 ½ years old. I went for interview after interview each one I was turned down, how I wish I had stayed on and pressed for o’levels. But of course itsit's too late to start wishing now.

Well I was bumingbooming of the government for a year and a half. And then after all that I was turned down for not being good enough at Maths. This is the time I started to ask. What is life for? Then after I had answered I started concentrating on getting a job.

I ended up in a local factory sewing seams together, then I fell in love, god when he walked in the door, I knew he had to be mine. We were married six months later cos I was in the club. I lost the baby and we were divorced 1 year later. He married again and now had 6 kids (4 grandchild).

I never remarried. I worked in shop, after shop, factory after factory. Then I decided to go away for a few weeks I had some cash in the bank and ended up in Portsmouth, staying in 1 star hotel. The manager was a nice, single, man as I got to know him I found his name was alanAlan Murray, we got serious and we married 2 years later. Then someone came up the road and opened a hotel that left us bankrupt and unhappy. We had 3 kids, Dave, Derek, Suzi.
We moved into a small flat in Battersea and lived bumingbooming of the dole, social security. I got a job cleaning a block of offices that paid £32.50 per two weeks.
Then one day I came home a bird of 18 in bed with Alan I did some screaming and shouting and she left and so did alan with his clothes.
We were divorced two years later. Later I heard he was killed in a car crash. I didnt even bother to mourn.
Dave came home saying he was getting married. We married and moved to Newcastle.
Derek was next to marry and moved to New ZealnadZealand.
Suzi stayed at home to look after her mum and soon became so attached to me that she didnt go out anywhere.
The years flew past and at 60 I had a heart attack and was left bedridden. Suzi carried on looking after me but was so ill she died.
I came here to the Old Folks Home where my 5 grandchildren visit me every other month.
Still there will come a day when they will have no one to visit.
